L1.area: Script compilation and bytecode generation pipeline

L2.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine's scope handling where a scope object is unconditionally instantiated during compilation even when it has no captured local variables. This incorrect instantiation leads to out-of-bounds memory access when the engine later processes the scope, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code via crafted JavaScript and gain the same privileges as the current user.

L3.area: Bytecode emission logic for function scope splitting and parameter scope management
L3.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine's bytecode emission phase where, during scope-splitting scenarios involving named function expressions with default parameter initializers, a parameter scope is unconditionally instantiated even when it contains no captured local variables. When only the function expression name is captured and resides in its own scope rather than the parameter scope, the unnecessary instantiation leads to out-of-bounds memory access. An attacker can exploit this via crafted JavaScript to gain arbitrary code execution with the current user's privileges.

ground_truth.reason: In split-scope scenarios, the parameter scope is unconditionally instantiated even when only the function expression symbol is captured and no formal parameters are in closure, leading to memory corruption exploitable for arbitrary code execution.
